authoritySources.citationNote: Existing source-inventory fixture evidence identifies inspection scheduling for online trade permits as a public DevHub service category.
Existing source-inventory fixture evidence covers public inspection scheduling guidance and must be consulted before any scheduling workflow is proposed.
Existing source-inventory fixture evidence covers public inspection-result follow-up guidance and result-dependent next steps.

processSkeleton.displayName: Inspection scheduling and result follow-up lifecycle skeleton
processSkeleton.scopeNote: Minimal fixture-only skeleton for inspection scheduling and inspection-result follow-up decisions. It uses existing source-inventory evidence references only and does not model a live DevHub session.

requiredFacts.label: Permit or request identifier
requiredFacts.neededFor: Match the inspection request or inspection result to the correct permit record.

requiredFacts.label: Inspection type
requiredFacts.neededFor: Determine whether the user is scheduling an inspection, checking a result, or following up after a result.

requiredFacts.label: Project contact and site access readiness
requiredFacts.neededFor: Confirm the user can provide required scheduling/contact/access details before any scheduling action is considered.

requiredFacts.label: Latest inspection result status
requiredFacts.neededFor: Route the user to closeout, correction, rescheduling, or PP&D contact follow-up after an inspection result.

requiredDocuments.label: Approved or relevant permit record reference
requiredDocuments.neededFor: Support inspection scheduling or result follow-up against the correct public or account-scoped permit record.
requiredDocuments.fixtureOnlyNote: This fixture does not include a real permit record, private account page, downloaded document, screenshot, or raw response body.

requiredDocuments.label: Correction or reinspection materials if required
requiredDocuments.neededFor: Represent result-dependent follow-up without automating an official correction upload or resubmission.
requiredDocuments.fixtureOnlyNote: Any official upload, certification, or resubmission is outside this fixture and must stop for exact user confirmation in a real guarded workflow.

userDecisions.prompt: Is the user trying to schedule an inspection, review an existing inspection result, or follow up after a result?
If scheduling through DevHub is proposed, has the user confirmed the permit is within an eligible online inspection scheduling scope?
After an inspection result, does the user need no action, correction work, rescheduling, or PP&D clarification?

actionGates.action: Read and summarize public PP&D inspection scheduling or result guidance.
Prepare a non-submitting preview of inspection scheduling facts, missing fields, and user decisions.
Schedule an inspection, select an official inspection time, or submit an inspection request in DevHub or another PP&D channel.
actionGates.stopGateMessage: Stop before scheduling or submitting an inspection request. The user must confirm the exact permit, inspection type, requested timing, contact/access details, and channel in the current session.

actionGates.action: Prepare a non-submitting result follow-up checklist from the known inspection result status.
Submit result follow-up, upload correction material, certify completion, request reinspection, or otherwise change an official permit or inspection record.
actionGates.stopGateMessage: Stop before any official inspection-result follow-up submission, upload, certification, or reinspection request. The user must confirm the exact action and supporting materials in the current session.

explicitStopGates: Do not schedule an inspection without exact session-specific user confirmation.
Do not submit an inspection request without exact session-specific user confirmation.
Do not upload correction materials or result follow-up documents from this fixture.
Do not certify, cancel, pay, or perform account-scoped DevHub actions from this fixture.
Do not automate CAPTCHA, MFA, account creation, payment entry, or any access-control bypass.
noLiveAutomationNotes: Fixture only: no live crawl, no DevHub login, no browser session, no authenticated page capture, and no official scheduling/submission action.
All IDs and citations are source-inventory references, not raw response bodies or downloaded documents.
Private user values, permit numbers, addresses, screenshots, traces, credentials, and storage-state files are intentionally absent.
